title: Sinningia × ornata
taxonomy: Sinningia × ornata is classified as a hybrid species within the Gesneriaceae family, which includes the African violet and other related plants. It belongs to the genus Sinningia, which consists of various flowering plants primarily found in South America. Sinningia × ornata is a cross between two Sinningia species, resulting in its unique characteristics. This hybrid is cultivated for its vibrant flowers and compact growth habit. Through its classification, Sinningia × ornata is recognized as a distinct and valuable addition to the world of ornamental plants.

intro: Sinningia × ornata, also known as the "Flamingo Flower" or "Gloxinia," is a hybrid plant species belonging to the family Gesneriaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Brazil, where it has been employed to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and respiratory problems, due to its potential calming and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Sinningia × ornata include alkaloids, particularly sinningine, which has been shown to possess anxiolytic and sedative properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
